summary: PR #2608 matched and linked src/MSL/math.c by changing MSL/math.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, moving/removing logf coefficient storage from math_data.c, adding .sdata2-local math constants in math.c, and reshaping logf codegen with tighter inlining and local expression changes. The bot report records 2 new matches: main/MSL/math .sdata2 to 100% and logf to 100%.

key_files.role: Changed MSL/math.c object classification from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk changing Object(NonMatching, "MSL/math.c") to Object(Matching, "MSL/math.c")

key_files.role: Primary matched function work for logf and associated .sdata2 constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/MSL/math.c hunks adding SECTION_SDATA2 constants and reshaping logf

key_files.role: Removed logf Taylor coefficient bit-pattern globals from math_data.c.

what_changed.text: configure.py now treats MSL/math.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
math.c adds SECTION_SDATA2, guarded for MWERKS and excluding M2CTX, then defines MSL_Math_804DE1B0, MSL_Math_804DE1B4, __float_nan, and __float_huge in .sdata2.
logf coefficient globals __logf_C0_bits and __logf_C1_bits were removed from math_data.c and replaced by MSL_Math_804DE1B0 and MSL_Math_804DE1B4 in math.c.

logf was reshaped by switching directly on the masked exponent expression, moving raw_fm/raw_fM declarations outward, using register float locals for H and h2_poly, inlining the polynomial expression, and explicitly casting E to float in LN2 multiplications.
NaN and infinity handling changed from float_nan/float_inf expression globals to __float_nan.f and __float_huge.f bit-pattern constants.
A stale block comment documenting a previous assembly mismatch for __one_over_F[m] was removed.

smart_moves.text: Kept MSL lookup tables in math_data.c while relocating only the logf coefficient and float special-value constants needed by this match, limiting the data change to math-related symbols visible in the diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/MSL/math.c extern lookup tables retained
raw_diff: src/MSL/math_data.c only coefficient definitions deleted

smart_moves.text: Used address-style names for newly introduced MSL_Math_804DE1B0 and MSL_Math_804DE1B4 rather than inventing semantic coefficient names not shown by the PR evidence.
The author identified that the last unmatched bit required inlining rather than further automated search.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small remaining MSL math mismatches, expression-level inlining can be decisive: this PR collapsed temporary polynomial locals into one expression and the author described the final fix as 'a bit of inlining.'

When matching MSL math data, constants may need precise section placement and bit-pattern representation rather than computed floating expressions; here NaN/huge and coefficient constants were represented with u32/FloatU32 data in .sdata2.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report showing main/MSL/math .sdata2 reached 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local data ownership can matter for a text match: logf coefficient bit-pattern globals were removed from math_data.c and defined in math.c with address-style MSL_Math names before math.c was linked as Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/MSL/math.c coefficient definitions and uses

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style MSL_Math_804DE1B0/MSL_Math_804DE1B4 names were used for newly added raw coefficient constants when semantic names were not retained.
__float_nan and __float_huge use double-underscore MSL-style special constant names and are accessed through the FloatU32 union field .f.
Temporary raw bit variables in logf use raw_* names such as raw_x, raw_fm, and raw_fM.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed object status to Matching only after the source/data edits; bot output reports logf and .sdata2 as new matches.
Used MWERKS-only __declspec(section ".sdata2") guarded away from M2CTX to place specific constants in .sdata2 without affecting non-MWERKS contexts.
Used u32 bit patterns plus FloatU32 reinterpretation for special floating values and coefficients to control loads and representations.
Introduced register float locals for H and h2_poly in the low-mantissa logf path.
Removed intermediate locals C0, C1, new_var, and poly by inlining the polynomial calculation into h2_poly.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says final bit required inlining

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 recorded Matched code +364 bytes, Linked code +364 bytes, Matched data +32 bytes, Linked data +32 bytes, with 2 new matches: main/MSL/math .sdata2 from 66.67% to 100.00% and logf from 99.80% to 100.00%.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: The changed files and exact diffs are fully loaded and not truncated.
The PR body gives direct author evidence that inlining solved the final mismatch, but does not specify every individual source edit's intent.
The bot report supports match/link progress and names logf and .sdata2 as new matches.
There are no loaded human review comments, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
No independent build or objdiff logs beyond the decomp-dev bot report are included in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inlining can close final MSL math expression mismatches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2608, the author stated that Codex could not crack the last bit and that it 'just required a bit of inlining'; the diff inlined logf polynomial temporaries into the h2_poly expression and the bot reported logf reaching 100%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: MSL math constants may require local .sdata2 bit-pattern placement
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2608 moved logf coefficient bit patterns out of math_data.c, added MWERKS .sdata2 constants in math.c, and the bot report showed main/MSL/math .sdata2 reaching 100%. Treat this as PR-specific evidence to check section ownership for MSL math constants rather than as a blanket rule to move constants.

source_update_candidates.title: src/MSL/math.c matched and linked by PR #2608
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2608 changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "MSL/math.c") to Object(Matching, "MSL/math.c"). The bot report recorded main/MSL/math logf reaching 100% and .sdata2 reaching 100%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-specific status fact for future MSL/math.c intake, but should be curated against current source before adding to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Register locals and section pragmas still need narrow match evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2608 used register float locals and a tightly guarded MWERKS .sdata2 section macro in a successful MSL math match. If curated, this should reinforce that such tactics are exceptions requiring bot/objdiff evidence and narrow scope, not general style.
source_update_candidates.reason: This aligns with existing standards on matching tactics and pragmas; curator may decide whether the PR provides enough example evidence to add as an illustrative note.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that constants should be moved from math_data.c into math.c; the evidence only covers logf coefficients and special values in this PR.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
Do not treat Codex failure as evidence about AI tools generally; the PR body only says Codex did not crack this specific final bit.
Do not promote register locals as normal style; the PR evidence only shows they were present in a successful narrow match.
